# UX Requirements Quality Checklist: GUI task fidelity

**Purpose**: Validate that the requirements governing what the operator sees and
can do — the editor's fidelity to a stored task, and group assignment across the
task list, editor, and group hierarchy — are complete, unambiguous, and
verifiable before implementation.
**Created**: 2026-07-22
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

**Depth**: Formal release gate · **Audience**: Reviewer (PR) · **Focus**: editor
fidelity, group assignment discoverability

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK031 Are requirements defined for every field the editor must populate from a stored task? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK032 Is the behavior specified for each mode the editor can open in? [Coverage, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK033 Are requirements defined for what the operator sees when the schedule lookup fails? [Coverage, Spec §FR-009]
- [x] CHK034 Are requirements defined for what is shown when no phrase is available but a schedule exists? [Completeness, Spec §FR-010]
- [x] CHK035 Is the required content of the group choice list specified, including the no-group choice? [Completeness, Spec §FR-012]
- [x] CHK036 Are requirements defined for where group membership must be visible outside the editor? [Coverage, Spec §FR-017, §FR-018]
- [x] CHK037 Are requirements defined for moving a task to a different group from the hierarchy view? [Completeness, Spec §FR-020]
- [x] CHK038 Does the spec state which actions must not act on a selected task? [Completeness, Spec §FR-021]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK039 Is "expressed in the task's timezone" (FR-007) unambiguous about which instant is displayed? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007]
- [x] CHK040 Is "visually distinguishable from groups" (FR-018) stated in a way a reviewer can judge? [Measurability, Spec §FR-018]
- [x] CHK041 Is "convey each group's position in the hierarchy" (FR-013) tied to a concrete discriminating case? [Clarity, Spec §FR-013]
- [x] CHK042 Is "clearly labeled ungrouped area" (FR-019) specific enough to prevent a bare or ambiguous label? [Clarity, Spec §FR-019]
- [x] CHK043 Is "treated as unmodified" (FR-008) defined by both of its observable consequences? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008]
- [ ] CHK044 Is the required message when a schedule cannot be read specified as actionable? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK045 Are the editor's group choices and the hierarchy view's move choices required to offer the same set? [Consistency, Spec §FR-012, §FR-020]
- [x] CHK046 Is group membership required to be presented consistently across the task list, the editor, and the hierarchy? [Consistency, Spec §FR-017, §FR-018, §FR-019a]
- [x] CHK047 Does the always-present ungrouped area (FR-019) stay consistent with the "every task appears exactly once" outcome? [Consistency, Spec §FR-019, §SC-005]
- [x] CHK048 Do the mode-switch requirement (FR-011b) and the blank-keeps-existing allowance (FR-008) have a stated precedence? [Conflict, Spec §FR-008, §FR-011b]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK049 Is "100% of tasks display their actual mode" (SC-001) verifiable across all three task origins it names? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001]
- [x] CHK050 Can "without leaving the GUI and without consulting documentation" (SC-003) be objectively assessed? [Measurability, Spec §SC-003]
- [x] CHK051 Is the one-second visibility target (SC-006) attached to a defined starting event? [Clarity, Spec §SC-006, §FR-022]
- [x] CHK052 Does every user story have at least one acceptance scenario that would fail today? [Traceability, Spec §User Scenarios]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK053 Are requirements defined for the primary flow of editing a task without changing its schedule? [Primary Flow, Spec §FR-008]
- [x] CHK054 Are requirements defined for the alternate flow of deliberately changing a schedule? [Alternate Flow, Spec §FR-011b]
- [x] CHK055 Are requirements defined for the exception flow where task detail cannot be read? [Exception Flow, Spec §FR-009]
- [x] CHK056 Are requirements defined for the recovery flow of correcting a wrong group assignment? [Recovery, Spec §User Story 3]
- [x] CHK057 Are zero-state requirements defined for a hierarchy with no groups, and for one with no ungrouped tasks? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-019]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- IDs continue from `migration.md` (CHK001–CHK030) so references stay globally unique
- An unchecked item at `/speckit-analyze` time is a finding, not a deferral
